codeReview.summary: CronRegistry implementation is solid with 25 passing tests. Uses croner crate for 6-field cron expression parsing and DashMap for thread-safe concurrent access. API design is clean with proper error handling via SwellError. All verification steps passed (cargo test, cargo clippy).

issues.description: CronEvent enum is defined and exported but never emitted anywhere - it is dead code. The enum variants (CronEntryRegistered, CronEntryRemoved, CronEntryUpdated, TaskDue) are not wired into any event system.

CronEntry::is_due() re-parses the cron expression on every invocation. This is inefficient when due_entries() iterates over many entries with the same expression. Consider caching the parsed Cron object.
CronEntry::next_scheduled() similarly re-parses the expression each call. If this method is used frequently, caching would improve performance.
CronEntry::is_due() is public but appears to be an internal helper method used only by due_entries(). Consider making it pub(crate) if it's not meant to be part of the public API.

sharedStateObservations.observation: CronEvent enum was defined but never integrated into any event emission system. The pattern in other registries (like TeamRegistry) shows events are broadcast via channels. This was apparently scoped but not completed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: CronEvent enum at cron_registry.rs:76 is never constructed or emitted anywhere in the codebase. TeamRegistry in team_registry.rs emits TeamTaskFailed events via broadcast channel.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-worker skill was used correctly. Worker followed TDD approach with 25 tests covering registration, due entries, updates, and edge cases. No procedure deviations noted.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true with no deviations. All 25 tests pass.

summary: CronRegistry implementation passes all checks. 25 tests verify registration, due_entries() behavior for various cron patterns (every-second, yearly Jan 1 midnight, etc.), entry updates, removal, concurrent access, and error cases. Implementation uses DashMap correctly for thread-safety and croner crate for cron parsing. Clippy clean. Four non-blocking issues noted: unused CronEvent enum (dead code), repeated cron expression parsing in is_due()/next_scheduled() (performance), and is_due() potentially being more public than intended. These do not block the feature from fulfilling its requirements.
